title: HAProxy Returning 503 Errors Due to Backend Server Pool Drain
symptoms: HAProxy returning 503 Service Unavailable for all requests
All backend servers showing DRAIN status in HAProxy stats page
HAProxy stats showing zero active backend servers
Maintenance script log showing all servers drained simultaneously
Backend servers healthy and responsive when accessed directly
root_cause: Maintenance automation script issued drain commands to all backend servers simultaneously instead of sequentially, leaving HAProxy with no available backends to serve traffic
resolution: Manually re-enabled all backend servers in HAProxy, killed the errant maintenance script, and fixed the script to process servers sequentially with health verification between each
preventive_actions: Fix maintenance script to drain servers one at a time with health verification
Implement minimum healthy backends guard preventing full pool drain
Add HAProxy backend availability monitoring with zero-backend alerting
Create maintenance window scheduling with change management approval
Implement HAProxy drain protection requiring manual override for last healthy backend

timeline.event: Maintenance script starts, drains all 8 backend servers simultaneously
HAProxy begins returning 503 with no available backends
Alert fires for 100% error rate on HAProxy
Alex Johnson investigates, sees all backends in DRAIN
Backend servers manually re-enabled in HAProxy
Traffic flowing normally, all backends active
Incident resolved, maintenance script fix scheduled

embedding_summary: HAProxy returning 503 errors after maintenance script simultaneously drained all 8 backend servers instead of sequentially. Complete on-premises service outage for 15 minutes affecting 4,500 internal users. Resolved by manually re-enabling backends and fixing the maintenance script.
